title: Segfault / negative-refcount: over-decreffed `sys.path` entry in `PyType_IsSubtype` (`typeobject.c:2931`)
description: When sys.path contains mortal entries and a module import runs under allocation failure, the path-based finder over-decrefs a sys.path entry. The freed/over-decreffed entry is then detected in one of two places: (segv) PathFinder._get_spec iterates sys.path and isinstance(entry, str) reads the entry's garbage ob_type -> PyType_IsSubtype derefs a->tp_mro on a bad pointer (typeobject.c:2931); or (abort) the next list slice-assignment decrefs the entry again as a recycled old element -> _Py_NegativeRefcount at list_ass_slice_lock_held (listobject.c:1030). Two faces of one over-decref. The vehicle reaches it via multiprocessing.forkserver._handle_preload, which does `sys.path[:] = sys_path` (a string -> a list of 1-char strings) then __import__ of each char.
notes: DETERMINISTIC minimal repro (repro.py, 6/6 ft_debug_asan): `sys.path[:] = '<unicode>'; __import__('H')` under the set_nomemory sweep -> _Py_NegativeRefcount at list_ass_slice_lock_held:1030. The import is REQUIRED -- `sys.path[:] = str` alone (no import) does NOT crash (5/5), so the over-decref producer is the import's path-based finder walking the new sys.path under OOM, not list_ass_slice itself; list_ass_slice:1030 is the recycle-cleanup decref that DETECTS the already-freed entry on the next slice op. The segv face (vehicle, PyType_IsSubtype:2931) is the same freed entry read by `isinstance(entry, str)` in _get_spec (importlib._bootstrap_external:1240). Small ints don't expose it (immortal); mortal entries (sys.path strings / 1-char strings) do. Root is PARTIAL: the exact over-decref line in the import path is not pinned, but the mechanism, both detection sites, and a deterministic repro are. This was the 'import-time PyType_IsSubtype outlier' that was NOREPRO on the host (catalog/norepro_investigation.md) -- now locally reproduced. Distinct from OOM-0004 (list_dealloc/PyList_New/free_list_items) and OOM-0019/0005/0029 (other negrefcount sites). Reached publicly via list slice-assignment + import under OOM; the vehicle's forkserver._handle_preload is one entry point (`sys.path[:] = sys_path` then __import__). Future: a testcase minimizer (shrinkray/creduce) + a reliable-repro bash loop could shrink vehicles like this automatically.
